evals.prompt: Run /zoto-spec-judge on the repository — no specific spec target. Assess the overall health of this codebase.
evals.expected_output: A repository assessment file at {specsDir}/assessment-repo-YYYYMMDD.md with scores across all six dimensions, specific findings, and actionable recommendations.
assertions: An assessment file is written to {specsDir}/assessment-repo-[yyyymmdd].md
All six dimensions are scored (Completeness, Feasibility, Structure, Specificity, Risk Awareness, Convention Compliance)
Each dimension has a numeric score between 1 and 5
An overall weighted score is computed
A verdict of Approve, Conditional, or Reject is stated
The Findings section includes both Strengths and Issues
Issues include severity levels (HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW)
Recommendations are actionable and specific to the repository

evals.prompt: Assess the spec at specs/20260401-api-migration/ — it has a spec index and 5 subtask files. Tell me if it's ready for execution.
evals.expected_output: A spec assessment file written into the spec directory with dimension scores, subtask manifest validation, dependency graph audit, and a clear verdict on execution readiness.
assertions: An assessment file is written into the spec directory following the naming convention assessment-[feature-name]-[yyyymmdd].md
The Subtask Manifest is validated: each listed file exists and metadata matches
The dependency graph is audited for correctness and missing edges
Each of the six dimensions has a score with specific findings
The report includes a Risk Summary table with likelihood and impact
A clear verdict (Approve, Conditional, or Reject) is given with reasoning
Subtask quality is evaluated: clear objectives, concrete deliverables, correct agents

evals.prompt: judge this spec — specs/20260402-auth-system/ — it's a draft and I want honest feedback before we refine it
evals.expected_output: A candid assessment that does not rubber-stamp the spec. Should identify real issues, score dimensions honestly, and provide specific recommendations for improvement.
assertions: The assessment does not give perfect 5/5 scores across all dimensions (genuine critical analysis)
At least one issue with severity HIGH or MEDIUM is identified if applicable
The Recommendation section gives 1-3 sentences on what to do next
Convention Compliance is evaluated against the repository's own patterns
The report format matches the template specified in the skill (Scores table, Findings, Dependency Graph, Risk Summary, Recommendation)
